---
title: "Funding"
description: "Supporting the development of EpiAware."
---

EpiAware is open-source and built in the open. Sustained development depends on
funding, and there are a few ways to help.

## Write us into a grant

If you are preparing a grant that will use or extend EpiAware, consider funding
part of that work through the ecosystem — for example a costed contribution to
maintenance, a specific package, or a component your project needs. This is one
of the most effective ways to keep the tools healthy while getting exactly the
functionality your work relies on. Get in touch early and we can help scope it.

## Collaborate

Funded collaborations — building a new package, adapting the tools to a
particular disease or setting, or supporting a response — are very welcome.
See [Get involved](get-involved.qmd) for how to start a conversation.

## Get in touch

We do not have a dedicated funding mechanism yet. To discuss supporting
EpiAware, contact
[Sam Abbott](https://www.lshtm.ac.uk/aboutus/people/abbott.sam) at LSHTM or open
an issue on [GitHub](https://github.com/EpiAware).
